entries.evidence_summary: Operative precedence listed as numbered layers (COO controller → review contracts → rulebook). Reference evidence placed under a separate 'Reference evidence (not operative authority)' heading. No flat subordinate_to list.

'Material pushback' defined canonically once in <guardrails>: 'any unresolved reviewer finding with blocking or major severity.' All other sections reference 'material pushback' with identical phrasing without re-defining it.

<outputs> groups artifacts under three explicitly labeled tables: 'Canonical package targets (promoted only on frozen or frozen_with_conditions)', 'Run-scoped per-round evidence', and 'Run-scoped terminal evidence'. Each row carries a class column ('canonical' or 'evidence') and full tool-relative path.

Five terminal states evaluated in fixed order with mutually exclusive testable predicates: blocked (unrecoverable error after escalation path), resume_required (budget exhausted AND material pushback), pushback (material pushback AND caller must supply changes), frozen_with_conditions (no material pushback AND deferred minor/suggestion only), frozen (no material pushback AND no deferred items).

Step 3 mandates 'semantic matching for scope and concept coverage rather than literal string matching, and record the matched evidence in the compliance map.' The self-check.json false positives (4 MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT errors for paths that ARE present in the <outputs> table at lines 181-184) are an implementation bug in the self-check tool using literal matching, not a role definition deficiency.

Five deduplicated, semantically distinct exclusions: tool creation; application workflow design; code implementation or runtime execution for other components; runtime orchestration outside the governed role-package review process; writes outside the governed surface. No duplicate or contradictory entries.

Trigger: split verdicts on same minor/suggestion persist for 2 consecutive rounds. Actor: the leader. Evidence: arbitration_used and arbitration_rationale in result.json (guardrails). Effect on terminal outcome: → frozen_with_conditions (Step 5 predicate 4 covers deferred arbitrated items).

All outputs use full tool-relative backtick-quoted paths (e.g., 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md'). The self-check.json MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT errors are false positives — those four canonical paths ARE present in the outputs table rows at lines 181-184 of the artifact.

All five terminal states have explicit 'complete when' predicates listing required artifacts: frozen (result.json + canonical promotion + run decision log + board summary + cycle post-mortem), frozen_with_conditions (same + deferred items listed), pushback (result.json + pushback.json + run evidence), blocked (result.json + bug-report.json + run evidence), resume_required (result.json + resume-package.json + run evidence).

<context-gathering> is labeled 'Preconditions before Step 1:' with 7 numbered precondition items. <steps> begins at 'Step 1. Validate runtime inputs and request'. No context-gathering items appear in the numbered execution flow.

Guardrails: 'Every carried-forward finding must be addressed by conceptual-group ID in the next round's fix items map as accepted or rejected with rationale.' Step 3: 'produce fix-items-map.json that addresses every carried-forward conceptual-group ID.' Step 4 sub-step 4: 'carry forward unresolved conceptual-group IDs explicitly.'

Preservation mechanism is fully specified across three locations. Guardrails: 'append a dated section to ...decision-log.md; never delete prior entries. Replace the canonical board summary only on promotion.' Step 5: 'append a dated section...instead of replacing prior history.' Outputs lifecycle column: 'update and fix: append a dated section on promotion; never delete prior entries.'

Steps preamble: 'All steps below describe current governed behavior required for active ADF role-package runs, not aspirational target-state behavior.' Each step header carries '(current governed behavior)' label.

Guardrails: 'Learning, compliance mapping, and diff evidence are required on every round where the inherited review flow says they apply; do not skip universal evidence because a revision path is closed.' This prevents gating of universal artifacts behind revision-specific conditions.

Single authoritative artifact matrix in <outputs> with three labeled tables. Each row declares: artifact name, class (canonical/evidence), full tool-relative path or root pattern, and write lifecycle specifying behavior per operation type (create/update/fix) and terminal state.

Authority names the exact shared sources (active review contracts, shared learning-engine review contract, review-process-architecture.md) in precedence order. Runtime obligations are 'inherited by reference' rather than restated locally. Component-local consequences only are defined inside the role package.

<role> states 'Your enduring mission is to create, update, and repair governed agent role packages...' — timeless standing purpose. No run state, resume status, or recovery action language present in canonical identity fields.

Write boundary anchored to COO/runtime contract in <authority>: 'The component write boundary is limited to tools/agent-role-builder/role/ and tools/agent-role-builder/runs/<job-id>/ unless a higher-order operative contract extends it.' Restated in guardrails with exact tool-relative paths. No implicit or abstract write surface.

Authority chain lists only operative sources (COO controller + runtime contract, active review contracts, active rulebook snapshot) under 'Operative precedence'. Static docs placed under 'Reference evidence (not operative authority)' — explicitly excluded from the binding authority order.

All governance mechanics (arbitration trigger/actor/evidence, terminal state predicates, review sequencing, learning engine placement) derive from or are inherited from the declared authority chain. No locally-invented board, leader, or terminal policy beyond what the authority set sanctions.
'Use when' and 'Owns' contain only inclusion statements. 'Not in scope' contains only exclusion statements. No positive clarifications embedded inside exclusion bullets, and no mixed-polarity items.

Step 4 declares 'Round n is an ordered sub-sequence:' with 7 sub-steps. Sub-step 1 names compliance-map.json and fix-items-map.json submitted to reviewers. Sub-step 2 names review.json. Sub-step 3 names learning.json. Sub-step 7 names run-postmortem.json and session-registry.json. All mandatory intra-round artifacts are bound to their write point.

<context-gathering> steps 2-3 list local governance files to load (review-prompt.json, review-contract.json, rulebook.json, shared review contract) with exact job-id-rooted paths. <authority> states 'Runtime obligations inherited by reference:' and names the governing docs rather than restating policy in full.

Required inputs enumerate: request JSON + source_refs, active reviewer roster, runtime review mode, component governance snapshots (review prompt, review contract, rulebook), active runtime configuration (review budget, watchdog timeout, launch attempts, promotion/write controls), active run identifier and run root, and current canonical package for update/fix operations.
